title: Nightfall — Initial Intrusion Analysis
subtitle: Spear-phishing to domain compromise across a 9-day dwell window.
body:
On 04 JUN the SOC observed anomalous OAuth consent grants against the finance tenant. Pivoting on the source ASN tied the activity to a known intrusion set tracked internally as NIGHTFALL.

Initial Access

Delivery was a tailored spear-phishing email impersonating a payroll vendor. The lure carried an HTML smuggling attachment that wrote a signed loader to disk.

"The operator waited 36 hours after foothold before the first hands-on-keyboard action — patience consistent with a mature crew."

Observed TTPs

  • T1566.001 — Spearphishing Attachment
  • T1027 — Obfuscated Files (HTML smuggling)
  • T1021.001 — Remote Services: RDP

Containment Snippet

Get-MailboxRule -Mailbox finance@corp | Where {$_.ForwardTo}
Revoke-AzureADUserAllRefreshToken -ObjectId <user>

Recommend immediate credential reset for the three impacted identities and conditional-access enforcement on legacy auth.

title: Nightfall — Indicators of Compromise
subtitle: Consolidated IOC set for blocklisting and threat hunting.
body:
The following indicators are high-confidence and cleared for blocklist deployment.

Network

185.225.73.21:443    Emotet C2
45.142.212.10:8080   QakBot C2
update-cdn-secure.com payload delivery

Hunting Query (KQL)

DeviceNetworkEvents
| where RemoteIP in ("185.225.73.21","45.142.212.10")
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, RemoteIP, RemotePort

Pair with the Initial Intrusion Analysis file for full narrative context.

title: Ransomware Triage Playbook
subtitle: First-hour actions for a suspected ransomware detonation.
body:
First 60 Minutes

  1. Isolate — pull affected hosts from the network, do not power off (preserve volatile memory).
  2. Identify the strain via ransom note + extension; check known-decryptor availability.
  3. Scope blast radius through EDR; pivot on the earliest detonation host.
Preserve a memory image before remediation — it is the single most valuable artifact and the first one lost.

Evidence Capture

winpmem -o mem.raw
veeam_restore --point pre-incident
